2224				   *retry_slot;
2225		ResultRelInfo *insert_destrel = NULL;
2226
2227		/*
2228		 * ExecCrossPartitionUpdate will first DELETE the row from the
2229		 * partition it's currently in and then insert it back into the root
2230		 * table, which will re-route it to the correct partition.  However,
2231		 * if the tuple has been concurrently updated, a retry is needed.
2232		 */
2233		if (ExecCrossPartitionUpdate(context, resultRelInfo,
2234									 tupleid, oldtuple, slot,
2235									 canSetTag, updateCxt,
2236									 &result,
2237									 &retry_slot,
2238									 &inserted_tuple,
2239									 &insert_destrel))
2240		{
2241			/* success! */
2242			updateCxt->crossPartUpdate = true;
2243
2244			/*
2245			 * If the partitioned table being updated is referenced in foreign
2246			 * keys, queue up trigger events to check that none of them were
2247			 * violated.  No special treatment is needed in
2248			 * non-cross-partition update situations, because the leaf
2249			 * partition's AR update triggers will take care of that.  During
2250			 * cross-partition updates implemented as delete on the source
2251			 * partition followed by insert on the destination partition,
2252			 * AR-UPDATE triggers of the root table (that is, the table
2253			 * mentioned in the query) must be fired.
2254			 *
2255			 * NULL insert_destrel means that the move failed to occur, that
2256			 * is, the update failed, so no need to anything in that case.
2257			 */
2258			if (insert_destrel &&
2259				resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc &&
2260				resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc->trig_update_after_row)
2261				ExecCrossPartitionUpdateForeignKey(context,
2262												   resultRelInfo,
2263												   insert_destrel,
2264												   tupleid, slot,
2265												   inserted_tuple);
2266
2267			return TM_Ok;
2268		}
2269
2270		/*
2271		 * No luck, a retry is needed.  If running MERGE, we do not do so
2272		 * here; instead let it handle that on its own rules.
2273		 */
2274		if (context->mtstate->operation == CMD_MERGE)
2275			return result;
2276
2277		/*
2278		 * ExecCrossPartitionUpdate installed an updated version of the new
2279		 * tuple in the retry slot; start over.
2280		 */
2281		slot = retry_slot;
2282		goto lreplace;
2283	}
2284
2285	/*
2286	 * Check the constraints of the tuple.  We've already checked the
2287	 * partition constraint above; however, we must still ensure the tuple
2288	 * passes all other constraints, so we will call ExecConstraints() and
2289	 * have it validate all remaining checks.
2290	 */
2291	if (resultRelationDesc->rd_att->constr)
2292		ExecConstraints(resultRelInfo, slot, estate);
2293
2294	/*
2295	 * replace the heap tuple
2296	 *
2297	 * Note: if es_crosscheck_snapshot isn't InvalidSnapshot, we check that
2298	 * the row to be updated is visible to that snapshot, and throw a
2299	 * can't-serialize error if not. This is a special-case behavior needed
2300	 * for referential integrity updates in transaction-snapshot mode
2301	 * transactions.
2302	 */
2303	result = table_tuple_update(resultRelationDesc, tupleid, slot,
2304								estate->es_output_cid,
2305								estate->es_snapshot,
2306								estate->es_crosscheck_snapshot,
2307								true /* wait for commit */ ,
2308								&context->tmfd, &updateCxt->lockmode,
2309								&updateCxt->updateIndexes);
2310
2311	return result;
2312}
2313
2314/*
2315 * ExecUpdateEpilogue -- subroutine for ExecUpdate
2316 *
2317 * Closing steps of updating a tuple.  Must be called if ExecUpdateAct
2318 * returns indicating that the tuple was updated.
2319 */
2320static void
2321ExecUpdateEpilogue(ModifyTableContext *context, UpdateContext *updateCxt,
2322				   ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo, ItemPointer tupleid,
2323				   HeapTuple oldtuple, TupleTableSlot *slot)
2324{
2325	ModifyTableState *mtstate = context->mtstate;
2326	List	   *recheckIndexes = NIL;
2327
2328	/* insert index entries for tuple if necessary */
2329	if (resultRelInfo->ri_NumIndices > 0 && (updateCxt->updateIndexes != TU_None))
2330		recheckIndexes = ExecInsertIndexTuples(resultRelInfo,
2331											   slot, context->estate,
2332											   true, false,
2333											   NULL, NIL,
2334											   (updateCxt->updateIndexes == TU_Summarizing));
2335
2336	/* AFTER ROW UPDATE Triggers */
2337	ExecARUpdateTriggers(context->estate, resultRelInfo,
2338						 NULL, NULL,
2339						 tupleid, oldtuple, slot,
2340						 recheckIndexes,
2341						 mtstate->operation == CMD_INSERT ?
2342						 mtstate->mt_oc_transition_capture :
2343						 mtstate->mt_transition_capture,
2344						 false);
2345
2346	list_free(recheckIndexes);
2347
2348	/*
2349	 * Check any WITH CHECK OPTION constraints from parent views.  We are
2350	 * required to do this after testing all constraints and uniqueness
2351	 * violations per the SQL spec, so we do it after actually updating the
2352	 * record in the heap and all indexes.
2353	 *
2354	 * ExecWithCheckOptions() will skip any WCOs which are not of the kind we
2355	 * are looking for at this point.
2356	 */
2357	if (resultRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptions != NIL)
2358		ExecWithCheckOptions(WCO_VIEW_CHECK, resultRelInfo,
2359							 slot, context->estate);
2360}
2361
2362/*
2363 * Queues up an update event using the target root partitioned table's
2364 * trigger to check that a cross-partition update hasn't broken any foreign
2365 * keys pointing into it.
2366 */
2367static void
2368ExecCrossPartitionUpdateForeignKey(ModifyTableContext *context,
2369								   ResultRelInfo *sourcePartInfo,
2370								   ResultRelInfo *destPartInfo,
2371								   ItemPointer tupleid,
2372								   TupleTableSlot *oldslot,
2373								   TupleTableSlot *newslot)
2374{
2375	ListCell   *lc;
2376	ResultRelInfo *rootRelInfo;
2377	List	   *ancestorRels;
2378
2379	rootRelInfo = sourcePartInfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo;
2380	ancestorRels = ExecGetAncestorResultRels(context->estate, sourcePartInfo);
2381
2382	/*
2383	 * For any foreign keys that point directly into a non-root ancestors of
2384	 * the source partition, we can in theory fire an update event to enforce
2385	 * those constraints using their triggers, if we could tell that both the
2386	 * source and the destination partitions are under the same ancestor. But
2387	 * for now, we simply report an error that those cannot be enforced.
2388	 */
2389	foreach(lc, ancestorRels)
2390	{
2391		ResultRelInfo *rInfo = lfirst(lc);
2392		TriggerDesc *trigdesc = rInfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2393		bool		has_noncloned_fkey = false;
2394
2395		/* Root ancestor's triggers will be processed. */
2396		if (rInfo == rootRelInfo)
2397			continue;
2398
2399		if (trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_update_after_row)
2400		{
2401			for (int i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2402			{
2403				Trigger    *trig = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2404
2405				if (!trig->tgisclone &&
2406					RI_FKey_trigger_type(trig->tgfoid) == RI_TRIGGER_PK)
2407				{
2408					has_noncloned_fkey = true;
2409					break;
2410				}
2411			}
2412		}
2413
2414		if (has_noncloned_fkey)
2415			ereport(ERROR,
2416					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2417					 errmsg("cannot move tuple across partitions when a non-root ancestor of the source partition is directly referenced in a foreign key"),
2418					 errdetail("A foreign key points to ancestor \"%s\" but not the root ancestor \"%s\".",
2419							   RelationGetRelationName(rInfo->ri_RelationDesc),
2420							   RelationGetRelationName(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc)),
2421					 errhint("Consider defining the foreign key on table \"%s\".",
2422							 RelationGetRelationName(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc))));
2423	}
2424
2425	/* Perform the root table's triggers. */
2426	ExecARUpdateTriggers(context->estate,
2427						 rootRelInfo, sourcePartInfo, destPartInfo,
2428						 tupleid, NULL, newslot, NIL, NULL, true);
2429}
2430
2431/* ----------------------------------------------------------------
2432 *		ExecUpdate
2433 *
2434 *		note: we can't run UPDATE queries with transactions
2435 *		off because UPDATEs are actually INSERTs and our
2436 *		scan will mistakenly loop forever, updating the tuple
2437 *		it just inserted..  This should be fixed but until it
2438 *		is, we don't want to get stuck in an infinite loop
2439 *		which corrupts your database..
2440 *
2441 *		When updating a table, tupleid identifies the tuple to update and
2442 *		oldtuple is NULL.  When updating through a view INSTEAD OF trigger,
2443 *		oldtuple is passed to the triggers and identifies what to update, and
2444 *		tupleid is invalid.  When updating a foreign table, tupleid is
2445 *		invalid; the FDW has to figure out which row to update using data from
2446 *		the planSlot.  oldtuple is passed to foreign table triggers; it is
2447 *		NULL when the foreign table has no relevant triggers.
2448 *
2449 *		oldSlot contains the old tuple value.
2450 *		slot contains the new tuple value to be stored.
2451 *		planSlot is the output of the ModifyTable's subplan; we use it
2452 *		to access values from other input tables (for RETURNING),
2453 *		row-ID junk columns, etc.
2454 *
2455 *		Returns RETURNING result if any, otherwise NULL.  On exit, if tupleid
2456 *		had identified the tuple to update, it will identify the tuple
2457 *		actually updated after EvalPlanQual.
2458 * ----------------------------------------------------------------
2459 */
2460static TupleTableSlot *
2461ExecUpdate(ModifyTableContext *context, ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
2462		   ItemPointer tupleid, HeapTuple oldtuple, TupleTableSlot *oldSlot,
2463		   TupleTableSlot *slot, bool canSetTag)
2464{
2465	EState	   *estate = context->estate;
2466	Relation	resultRelationDesc = resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2467	UpdateContext updateCxt = {0};
2468	TM_Result	result;
2469
2470	/*
2471	 * abort the operation if not running transactions
2472	 */
2473	if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
2474		elog(ERROR, "cannot UPDATE during bootstrap");
2475
2476	/*
2477	 * Prepare for the update.  This includes BEFORE ROW triggers, so we're
2478	 * done if it says we are.
2479	 */
2480	if (!ExecUpdatePrologue(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid, oldtuple, slot, NULL))
2481		return NULL;
2482
2483	/* INSTEAD OF ROW UPDATE Triggers */
2484	if (resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc &&
2485		resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
2486	{
2487		if (!ExecIRUpdateTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo,
2488								  oldtuple, slot))
2489			return NULL;		/* "do nothing" */
2490	}
2491	else if (resultRelInfo->ri_FdwRoutine)
2492	{
2493		/* Fill in GENERATEd columns */
2494		ExecUpdatePrepareSlot(resultRelInfo, slot, estate);
2495
2496		/*
2497		 * update in foreign table: let the FDW do it
2498		 */
2499		slot = resultRelInfo->ri_FdwRoutine->ExecForeignUpdate(estate,
2500															   resultRelInfo,
2501															   slot,
2502															   context->planSlot);
2503
2504		if (slot == NULL)		/* "do nothing" */
2505			return NULL;
2506
2507		/*
2508		 * AFTER ROW Triggers or RETURNING expressions might reference the
2509		 * tableoid column, so (re-)initialize tts_tableOid before evaluating
2510		 * them.  (This covers the case where the FDW replaced the slot.)
2511		 */
2512		slot->tts_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(resultRelationDesc);
2513	}
2514	else
2515	{
2516		ItemPointerData lockedtid;
2517
2518		/*
2519		 * If we generate a new candidate tuple after EvalPlanQual testing, we
2520		 * must loop back here to try again.  (We don't need to redo triggers,
2521		 * however.  If there are any BEFORE triggers then trigger.c will have
2522		 * done table_tuple_lock to lock the correct tuple, so there's no need
2523		 * to do them again.)
2524		 */
2525redo_act:
2526		lockedtid = *tupleid;
2527		result = ExecUpdateAct(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid, oldtuple, slot,
2528							   canSetTag, &updateCxt);
2529
2530		/*
2531		 * If ExecUpdateAct reports that a cross-partition update was done,
2532		 * then the RETURNING tuple (if any) has been projected and there's
2533		 * nothing else for us to do.
2534		 */
2535		if (updateCxt.crossPartUpdate)
2536			return context->cpUpdateReturningSlot;
2537
2538		switch (result)
2539		{
2540			case TM_SelfModified:
2541
2542				/*
2543				 * The target tuple was already updated or deleted by the
2544				 * current command, or by a later command in the current
2545				 * transaction.  The former case is possible in a join UPDATE
2546				 * where multiple tuples join to the same target tuple. This
2547				 * is pretty questionable, but Postgres has always allowed it:
2548				 * we just execute the first update action and ignore
2549				 * additional update attempts.
2550				 *
2551				 * The latter case arises if the tuple is modified by a
2552				 * command in a BEFORE trigger, or perhaps by a command in a
2553				 * volatile function used in the query.  In such situations we
2554				 * should not ignore the update, but it is equally unsafe to
2555				 * proceed.  We don't want to discard the original UPDATE
2556				 * while keeping the triggered actions based on it; and we
2557				 * have no principled way to merge this update with the
2558				 * previous ones.  So throwing an error is the only safe
2559				 * course.
2560				 *
2561				 * If a trigger actually intends this type of interaction, it
2562				 * can re-execute the UPDATE (assuming it can figure out how)
2563				 * and then return NULL to cancel the outer update.
2564				 */
2565				if (context->tmfd.cmax != estate->es_output_cid)
2566					ereport(ERROR,
2567							(errcode(ERRCODE_TRIGGERED_DATA_CHANGE_VIOLATION),
2568							 errmsg("tuple to be updated was already modified by an operation triggered by the current command"),
2569							 errhint("Consider using an AFTER trigger instead of a BEFORE trigger to propagate changes to other rows.")));
2570
2571				/* Else, already updated by self; nothing to do */
2572				return NULL;
2573
2574			case TM_Ok:
2575				break;
2576
2577			case TM_Updated:
2578				{
2579					TupleTableSlot *inputslot;
2580					TupleTableSlot *epqslot;
2581
2582					if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
2583						ereport(ERROR,
2584								(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
2585								 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent update")));
2586
2587					/*
2588					 * Already know that we're going to need to do EPQ, so
2589					 * fetch tuple directly into the right slot.
2590					 */
2591					inputslot = EvalPlanQualSlot(context->epqstate, resultRelationDesc,
2592												 resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex);
2593
2594					result = table_tuple_lock(resultRelationDesc, tupleid,
2595											  estate->es_snapshot,
2596											  inputslot, estate->es_output_cid,
2597											  updateCxt.lockmode, LockWaitBlock,
2598											  TUPLE_LOCK_FLAG_FIND_LAST_VERSION,
2599											  &context->tmfd);
2600
2601					switch (result)
2602					{
2603						case TM_Ok:
2604							Assert(context->tmfd.traversed);
2605
2606							epqslot = EvalPlanQual(context->epqstate,
2607												   resultRelationDesc,
2608												   resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex,
2609												   inputslot);
2610							if (TupIsNull(epqslot))
2611								/* Tuple not passing quals anymore, exiting... */
2612								return NULL;
2613
2614							/* Make sure ri_oldTupleSlot is initialized. */
2615							if (unlikely(!resultRelInfo->ri_projectNewInfoValid))
2616								ExecInitUpdateProjection(context->mtstate,
2617														 resultRelInfo);
2618
2619							if (resultRelInfo->ri_needLockTagTuple)
2620							{
2621								UnlockTuple(resultRelationDesc,
2622											&lockedtid, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
2623								LockTuple(resultRelationDesc,
2624										  tupleid, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
2625							}
2626
2627							/* Fetch the most recent version of old tuple. */
2628							oldSlot = resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot;
2629							if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(resultRelationDesc,
2630															   tupleid,
2631															   SnapshotAny,
2632															   oldSlot))
2633								elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple being updated");
2634							slot = ExecGetUpdateNewTuple(resultRelInfo,
2635														 epqslot, oldSlot);
2636							goto redo_act;
2637
2638						case TM_Deleted:
2639							/* tuple already deleted; nothing to do */
2640							return NULL;
2641
2642						case TM_SelfModified:
2643
2644							/*
2645							 * This can be reached when following an update
2646							 * chain from a tuple updated by another session,
2647							 * reaching a tuple that was already updated in
2648							 * this transaction. If previously modified by
2649							 * this command, ignore the redundant update,
2650							 * otherwise error out.
2651							 *
2652							 * See also TM_SelfModified response to
2653							 * table_tuple_update() above.
2654							 */
2655							if (context->tmfd.cmax != estate->es_output_cid)
2656								ereport(ERROR,
2657										(errcode(ERRCODE_TRIGGERED_DATA_CHANGE_VIOLATION),
2658										 errmsg("tuple to be updated was already modified by an operation triggered by the current command"),
2659										 errhint("Consider using an AFTER trigger instead of a BEFORE trigger to propagate changes to other rows.")));
2660							return NULL;
2661
2662						default:
2663							/* see table_tuple_lock call in ExecDelete() */
2664							elog(ERROR, "unexpected table_tuple_lock status: %u",
2665								 result);
2666							return NULL;
2667					}
2668				}
2669
2670				break;
2671
2672			case TM_Deleted:
2673				if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
2674					ereport(ERROR,
2675							(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
2676							 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent delete")));
2677				/* tuple already deleted; nothing to do */
2678				return NULL;
2679
2680			default:
2681				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized table_tuple_update status: %u",
2682					 result);
2683				return NULL;
2684		}
2685	}
2686
2687	if (canSetTag)
2688		(estate->es_processed)++;
2689
2690	ExecUpdateEpilogue(context, &updateCxt, resultRelInfo, tupleid, oldtuple,
2691					   slot);
2692
2693	/* Process RETURNING if present */
2694	if (resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning)
2695		return ExecProcessReturning(context, resultRelInfo, CMD_UPDATE,
2696									oldSlot, slot, context->planSlot);
2697
2698	return NULL;
2699}
2700
2701/*
2702 * ExecOnConflictUpdate --- execute UPDATE of INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE
2703 *
2704 * Try to lock tuple for update as part of speculative insertion.  If
2705 * a qual originating from ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE is satisfied, update
2706 * (but still lock row, even though it may not satisfy estate's
2707 * snapshot).
2708 *
2709 * Returns true if we're done (with or without an update), or false if
2710 * the caller must retry the INSERT from scratch.
2711 */
2712static bool
2713ExecOnConflictUpdate(ModifyTableContext *context,
2714					 ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
2715					 ItemPointer conflictTid,
2716					 TupleTableSlot *excludedSlot,
2717					 bool canSetTag,
2718					 TupleTableSlot **returning)
2719{
2720	ModifyTableState *mtstate = context->mtstate;
2721	ExprContext *econtext = mtstate->ps.ps_ExprContext;
2722	Relation	relation = resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2723	ExprState  *onConflictSetWhere = resultRelInfo->ri_onConflict->oc_WhereClause;
2724	TupleTableSlot *existing = resultRelInfo->ri_onConflict->oc_Existing;
2725	TM_FailureData tmfd;
2726	LockTupleMode lockmode;
2727	TM_Result	test;
2728	Datum		xminDatum;
2729	TransactionId xmin;
2730	bool		isnull;
2731
2732	/*
2733	 * Parse analysis should have blocked ON CONFLICT for all system
2734	 * relations, which includes these.  There's no fundamental obstacle to
2735	 * supporting this; we'd just need to handle LOCKTAG_TUPLE like the other
2736	 * ExecUpdate() caller.
2737	 */
2738	Assert(!resultRelInfo->ri_needLockTagTuple);
2739
2740	/* Determine lock mode to use */
2741	lockmode = ExecUpdateLockMode(context->estate, resultRelInfo);
2742
2743	/*
2744	 * Lock tuple for update.  Don't follow updates when tuple cannot be
2745	 * locked without doing so.  A row locking conflict here means our
2746	 * previous conclusion that the tuple is conclusively committed is not
2747	 * true anymore.
2748	 */
2749	test = table_tuple_lock(relation, conflictTid,
2750							context->estate->es_snapshot,
2751							existing, context->estate->es_output_cid,
2752							lockmode, LockWaitBlock, 0,
2753							&tmfd);
2754	switch (test)
2755	{
2756		case TM_Ok:
2757			/* success! */
2758			break;
2759
2760		case TM_Invisible:
2761
2762			/*
2763			 * This can occur when a just inserted tuple is updated again in
2764			 * the same command. E.g. because multiple rows with the same
2765			 * conflicting key values are inserted.
2766			 *
2767			 * This is somewhat similar to the ExecUpdate() TM_SelfModified
2768			 * case.  We do not want to proceed because it would lead to the
2769			 * same row being updated a second time in some unspecified order,
2770			 * and in contrast to plain UPDATEs there's no historical behavior
2771			 * to break.
2772			 *
2773			 * It is the user's responsibility to prevent this situation from
2774			 * occurring.  These problems are why the SQL standard similarly
2775			 * specifies that for SQL MERGE, an exception must be raised in
2776			 * the event of an attempt to update the same row twice.
2777			 */
2778			xminDatum = slot_getsysattr(existing,
2779										MinTransactionIdAttributeNumber,
2780										&isnull);
2781			Assert(!isnull);
2782			xmin = DatumGetTransactionId(xminDatum);
2783
2784			if (TransactionIdIsCurrentTransactionId(xmin))
2785				ereport(ERROR,
2786						(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
2787				/* translator: %s is a SQL command name */
2788						 errmsg("%s command cannot affect row a second time",
2789								"ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE"),
2790						 errhint("Ensure that no rows proposed for insertion within the same command have duplicate constrained values.")));
2791
2792			/* This shouldn't happen */
2793			elog(ERROR, "attempted to lock invisible tuple");
2794			break;
2795
2796		case TM_SelfModified:
2797
2798			/*
2799			 * This state should never be reached. As a dirty snapshot is used
2800			 * to find conflicting tuples, speculative insertion wouldn't have
2801			 * seen this row to conflict with.
2802			 */
2803			elog(ERROR, "unexpected self-updated tuple");
2804			break;
2805
2806		case TM_Updated:
2807			if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
2808				ereport(ERROR,
2809						(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
2810						 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent update")));
2811
2812			/*
2813			 * Tell caller to try again from the very start.
2814			 *
2815			 * It does not make sense to use the usual EvalPlanQual() style
2816			 * loop here, as the new version of the row might not conflict
2817			 * anymore, or the conflicting tuple has actually been deleted.
2818			 */
2819			ExecClearTuple(existing);
2820			return false;
2821
2822		case TM_Deleted:
2823			if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
2824				ereport(ERROR,
2825						(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
2826						 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent delete")));
2827
2828			/* see TM_Updated case */
2829			ExecClearTuple(existing);
2830			return false;
2831
2832		default:
2833			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized table_tuple_lock status: %u", test);
2834	}
2835
2836	/* Success, the tuple is locked. */
2837
2838	/*
2839	 * Verify that the tuple is visible to our MVCC snapshot if the current
2840	 * isolation level mandates that.
2841	 *
2842	 * It's not sufficient to rely on the check within ExecUpdate() as e.g.
2843	 * CONFLICT ... WHERE clause may prevent us from reaching that.
2844	 *
2845	 * This means we only ever continue when a new command in the current
2846	 * transaction could see the row, even though in READ COMMITTED mode the
2847	 * tuple will not be visible according to the current statement's
2848	 * snapshot.  This is in line with the way UPDATE deals with newer tuple
2849	 * versions.
2850	 */
2851	ExecCheckTupleVisible(context->estate, relation, existing);
2852
2853	/*
2854	 * Make tuple and any needed join variables available to ExecQual and
2855	 * ExecProject.  The EXCLUDED tuple is installed in ecxt_innertuple, while
2856	 * the target's existing tuple is installed in the scantuple.  EXCLUDED
2857	 * has been made to reference INNER_VAR in setrefs.c, but there is no
2858	 * other redirection.
2859	 */
2860	econtext->ecxt_scantuple = existing;
2861	econtext->ecxt_innertuple = excludedSlot;
2862	econtext->ecxt_outertuple = NULL;
2863
2864	if (!ExecQual(onConflictSetWhere, econtext))
2865	{
2866		ExecClearTuple(existing);	/* see return below */
2867		InstrCountFiltered1(&mtstate->ps, 1);
2868		return true;			/* done with the tuple */
2869	}
2870
2871	if (resultRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptions != NIL)
2872	{
2873		/*
2874		 * Check target's existing tuple against UPDATE-applicable USING
2875		 * security barrier quals (if any), enforced here as RLS checks/WCOs.
2876		 *
2877		 * The rewriter creates UPDATE RLS checks/WCOs for UPDATE security
2878		 * quals, and stores them as WCOs of "kind" WCO_RLS_CONFLICT_CHECK,
2879		 * but that's almost the extent of its special handling for ON
2880		 * CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
2881		 *
2882		 * The rewriter will also have associated UPDATE applicable straight
2883		 * RLS checks/WCOs for the benefit of the ExecUpdate() call that
2884		 * follows.  INSERTs and UPDATEs naturally have mutually exclusive WCO
2885		 * kinds, so there is no danger of spurious over-enforcement in the
2886		 * INSERT or UPDATE path.
2887		 */
2888		ExecWithCheckOptions(WCO_RLS_CONFLICT_CHECK, resultRelInfo,
2889							 existing,
2890							 mtstate->ps.state);
2891	}
2892
2893	/* Project the new tuple version */
2894	ExecProject(resultRelInfo->ri_onConflict->oc_ProjInfo);
2895
2896	/*
2897	 * Note that it is possible that the target tuple has been modified in
2898	 * this session, after the above table_tuple_lock. We choose to not error
2899	 * out in that case, in line with ExecUpdate's treatment of similar cases.
2900	 * This can happen if an UPDATE is triggered from within ExecQual(),
2901	 * ExecWithCheckOptions() or ExecProject() above, e.g. by selecting from a
2902	 * wCTE in the ON CONFLICT's SET.
2903	 */
2904
2905	/* Execute UPDATE with projection */
2906	*returning = ExecUpdate(context, resultRelInfo,
2907							conflictTid, NULL, existing,
2908							resultRelInfo->ri_onConflict->oc_ProjSlot,
2909							canSetTag);
2910
2911	/*
2912	 * Clear out existing tuple, as there might not be another conflict among
2913	 * the next input rows. Don't want to hold resources till the end of the
2914	 * query.  First though, make sure that the returning slot, if any, has a
2915	 * local copy of any OLD pass-by-reference values, if it refers to any OLD
2916	 * columns.
2917	 */
2918	if (*returning != NULL &&
2919		resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning->pi_state.flags & EEO_FLAG_HAS_OLD)
2920		ExecMaterializeSlot(*returning);
2921
2922	ExecClearTuple(existing);
2923
2924	return true;
2925}
2926
2927/*
2928 * Perform MERGE.
2929 */
2930static TupleTableSlot *
2931ExecMerge(ModifyTableContext *context, ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
2932		  ItemPointer tupleid, HeapTuple oldtuple, bool canSetTag)
2933{
2934	TupleTableSlot *rslot = NULL;
2935	bool		matched;
2936
2937	/*-----
2938	 * If we are dealing with a WHEN MATCHED case, tupleid or oldtuple is
2939	 * valid, depending on whether the result relation is a table or a view.
2940	 * We execute the first action for which the additional WHEN MATCHED AND
2941	 * quals pass.  If an action without quals is found, that action is
2942	 * executed.
2943	 *
2944	 * Similarly, in the WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE case, tupleid or oldtuple
2945	 * is valid, and we look at the given WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE actions
2946	 * in sequence until one passes.  This is almost identical to the WHEN
2947	 * MATCHED case, and both cases are handled by ExecMergeMatched().
2948	 *
2949	 * Finally, in the WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] case, both tupleid and
2950	 * oldtuple are invalid, and we look at the given WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY
2951	 * TARGET] actions in sequence until one passes.
2952	 *
2953	 * Things get interesting in case of concurrent update/delete of the
2954	 * target tuple. Such concurrent update/delete is detected while we are
2955	 * executing a WHEN MATCHED or WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE action.
2956	 *
2957	 * A concurrent update can:
2958	 *
2959	 * 1. modify the target tuple so that the results from checking any
2960	 *    additional quals attached to WHEN MATCHED or WHEN NOT MATCHED BY
2961	 *    SOURCE actions potentially change, but the result from the join
2962	 *    quals does not change.
2963	 *
2964	 *    In this case, we are still dealing with the same kind of match
2965	 *    (MATCHED or NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE).  We recheck the same list of
2966	 *    actions from the start and choose the first one that satisfies the
2967	 *    new target tuple.
2968	 *
2969	 * 2. modify the target tuple in the WHEN MATCHED case so that the join
2970	 *    quals no longer pass and hence the source and target tuples no
2971	 *    longer match.
2972	 *
2973	 *    In this case, we are now dealing with a NOT MATCHED case, and we
2974	 *    process both WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE and WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY
2975	 *    TARGET] actions.  First ExecMergeMatched() processes the list of
2976	 *    WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE actions in sequence until one passes,
2977	 *    then ExecMergeNotMatched() processes any WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY
2978	 *    TARGET] actions in sequence until one passes.  Thus we may execute
2979	 *    two actions; one of each kind.
2980	 *
2981	 * Thus we support concurrent updates that turn MATCHED candidate rows
2982	 * into NOT MATCHED rows.  However, we do not attempt to support cases
2983	 * that would turn NOT MATCHED rows into MATCHED rows, or which would
2984	 * cause a target row to match a different source row.
2985	 *
2986	 * A concurrent delete changes a WHEN MATCHED case to WHEN NOT MATCHED
2987	 * [BY TARGET].
2988	 *
2989	 * ExecMergeMatched() takes care of following the update chain and
2990	 * re-finding the qualifying WHEN MATCHED or WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE
2991	 * action, as long as the target tuple still exists. If the target tuple
2992	 * gets deleted or a concurrent update causes the join quals to fail, it
2993	 * returns a matched status of false and we call ExecMergeNotMatched().
2994	 * Given that ExecMergeMatched() always makes progress by following the
2995	 * update chain and we never switch from ExecMergeNotMatched() to
2996	 * ExecMergeMatched(), there is no risk of a livelock.
2997	 */
2998	matched = tupleid != NULL || oldtuple != NULL;
2999	if (matched)
3000		rslot = ExecMergeMatched(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid, oldtuple,
3001								 canSetTag, &matched);
3002
3003	/*
3004	 * Deal with the NOT MATCHED case (either a NOT MATCHED tuple from the
3005	 * join, or a previously MATCHED tuple for which ExecMergeMatched() set
3006	 * "matched" to false, indicating that it no longer matches).
3007	 */
3008	if (!matched)
3009	{
3010		/*
3011		 * If a concurrent update turned a MATCHED case into a NOT MATCHED
3012		 * case, and we have both WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE and WHEN NOT
3013		 * MATCHED [BY TARGET] actions, and there is a RETURNING clause,
3014		 * ExecMergeMatched() may have already executed a WHEN NOT MATCHED BY
3015		 * SOURCE action, and computed the row to return.  If so, we cannot
3016		 * execute a WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] action now, so mark it as
3017		 * pending (to be processed on the next call to ExecModifyTable()).
3018		 * Otherwise, just process the action now.
3019		 */
3020		if (rslot == NULL)
3021			rslot = ExecMergeNotMatched(context, resultRelInfo, canSetTag);
3022		else
3023			context->mtstate->mt_merge_pending_not_matched = context->planSlot;
3024	}
3025
3026	return rslot;
3027}
3028
3029/*
3030 * Check and execute the first qualifying MATCHED or NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE
3031 * action, depending on whether the join quals are satisfied.  If the target
3032 * relation is a table, the current target tuple is identified by tupleid.
3033 * Otherwise, if the target relation is a view, oldtuple is the current target
3034 * tuple from the view.
3035 *
3036 * We start from the first WHEN MATCHED or WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE action
3037 * and check if the WHEN quals pass, if any. If the WHEN quals for the first
3038 * action do not pass, we check the second, then the third and so on. If we
3039 * reach the end without finding a qualifying action, we return NULL.
3040 * Otherwise, we execute the qualifying action and return its RETURNING
3041 * result, if any, or NULL.
3042 *
3043 * On entry, "*matched" is assumed to be true.  If a concurrent update or
3044 * delete is detected that causes the join quals to no longer pass, we set it
3045 * to false, indicating that the caller should process any NOT MATCHED [BY
3046 * TARGET] actions.
3047 *
3048 * After a concurrent update, we restart from the first action to look for a
3049 * new qualifying action to execute. If the join quals originally passed, and
3050 * the concurrent update caused them to no longer pass, then we switch from
3051 * the MATCHED to the NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE list of actions before restarting
3052 * (and setting "*matched" to false).  As a result we may execute a WHEN NOT
3053 * MATCHED BY SOURCE action, and set "*matched" to false, causing the caller
3054 * to also execute a WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] action.
3055 */
3056static TupleTableSlot *
3057ExecMergeMatched(ModifyTableContext *context, ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
3058				 ItemPointer tupleid, HeapTuple oldtuple, bool canSetTag,
3059				 bool *matched)
3060{
3061	ModifyTableState *mtstate = context->mtstate;
3062	List	  **mergeActions = resultRelInfo->ri_MergeActions;
3063	ItemPointerData lockedtid;
3064	List	   *actionStates;
3065	TupleTableSlot *newslot = NULL;
3066	TupleTableSlot *rslot = NULL;
3067	EState	   *estate = context->estate;
3068	ExprContext *econtext = mtstate->ps.ps_ExprContext;
3069	bool		isNull;
3070	EPQState   *epqstate = &mtstate->mt_epqstate;
3071	ListCell   *l;
3072
3073	/* Expect matched to be true on entry */
3074	Assert(*matched);
3075
3076	/*
3077	 * If there are no WHEN MATCHED or WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE actions, we
3078	 * are done.
3079	 */
3080	if (mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_MATCHED] == NIL &&
3081		mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE] == NIL)
3082		return NULL;
3083
3084	/*
3085	 * Make tuple and any needed join variables available to ExecQual and
3086	 * ExecProject. The target's existing tuple is installed in the scantuple.
3087	 * This target relation's slot is required only in the case of a MATCHED
3088	 * or NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE tuple and UPDATE/DELETE actions.
3089	 */
3090	econtext->ecxt_scantuple = resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot;
3091	econtext->ecxt_innertuple = context->planSlot;
3092	econtext->ecxt_outertuple = NULL;
3093
3094	/*
3095	 * This routine is only invoked for matched target rows, so we should
3096	 * either have the tupleid of the target row, or an old tuple from the
3097	 * target wholerow junk attr.
3098	 */
3099	Assert(tupleid != NULL || oldtuple != NULL);
3100	ItemPointerSetInvalid(&lockedtid);
3101	if (oldtuple != NULL)
3102	{
3103		Assert(!resultRelInfo->ri_needLockTagTuple);
3104		ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(oldtuple, resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot,
3105								false);
3106	}
3107	else
3108	{
3109		if (resultRelInfo->ri_needLockTagTuple)
3110		{
3111			/*
3112			 * This locks even for CMD_DELETE, for CMD_NOTHING, and for tuples
3113			 * that don't match mas_whenqual.  MERGE on system catalogs is a
3114			 * minor use case, so don't bother optimizing those.
3115			 */
3116			LockTuple(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc, tupleid,
3117					  InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
3118			lockedtid = *tupleid;
3119		}
3120		if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3121										   tupleid,
3122										   SnapshotAny,
3123										   resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot))
3124			elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch the target tuple");
3125	}
3126
3127	/*
3128	 * Test the join condition.  If it's satisfied, perform a MATCHED action.
3129	 * Otherwise, perform a NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE action.
3130	 *
3131	 * Note that this join condition will be NULL if there are no NOT MATCHED
3132	 * BY SOURCE actions --- see transform_MERGE_to_join().  In that case, we
3133	 * need only consider MATCHED actions here.
3134	 */
3135	if (ExecQual(resultRelInfo->ri_MergeJoinCondition, econtext))
3136		actionStates = mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_MATCHED];
3137	else
3138		actionStates = mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE];
3139
3140lmerge_matched:
3141
3142	foreach(l, actionStates)
3143	{
3144		MergeActionState *relaction = (MergeActionState *) lfirst(l);
3145		CmdType		commandType = relaction->mas_action->commandType;
3146		TM_Result	result;
3147		UpdateContext updateCxt = {0};
3148
3149		/*
3150		 * Test condition, if any.
3151		 *
3152		 * In the absence of any condition, we perform the action
3153		 * unconditionally (no need to check separately since ExecQual() will
3154		 * return true if there are no conditions to evaluate).
3155		 */
3156		if (!ExecQual(relaction->mas_whenqual, econtext))
3157			continue;
3158
3159		/*
3160		 * Check if the existing target tuple meets the USING checks of
3161		 * UPDATE/DELETE RLS policies. If those checks fail, we throw an
3162		 * error.
3163		 *
3164		 * The WITH CHECK quals for UPDATE RLS policies are applied in
3165		 * ExecUpdateAct() and hence we need not do anything special to handle
3166		 * them.
3167		 *
3168		 * NOTE: We must do this after WHEN quals are evaluated, so that we
3169		 * check policies only when they matter.
3170		 */
3171		if (resultRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptions && commandType != CMD_NOTHING)
3172		{
3173			ExecWithCheckOptions(commandType == CMD_UPDATE ?
3174								 WCO_RLS_MERGE_UPDATE_CHECK : WCO_RLS_MERGE_DELETE_CHECK,
3175								 resultRelInfo,
3176								 resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot,
3177								 context->mtstate->ps.state);
3178		}
3179
3180		/* Perform stated action */
3181		switch (commandType)
3182		{
3183			case CMD_UPDATE:
3184
3185				/*
3186				 * Project the output tuple, and use that to update the table.
3187				 * We don't need to filter out junk attributes, because the
3188				 * UPDATE action's targetlist doesn't have any.
3189				 */
3190				newslot = ExecProject(relaction->mas_proj);
3191
3192				mtstate->mt_merge_action = relaction;
3193				if (!ExecUpdatePrologue(context, resultRelInfo,
3194										tupleid, NULL, newslot, &result))
3195				{
3196					if (result == TM_Ok)
3197						goto out;	/* "do nothing" */
3198
3199					break;		/* concurrent update/delete */
3200				}
3201
3202				/* INSTEAD OF ROW UPDATE Triggers */
3203				if (resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc &&
3204					resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
3205				{
3206					if (!ExecIRUpdateTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo,
3207											  oldtuple, newslot))
3208						goto out;	/* "do nothing" */
3209				}
3210				else
3211				{
3212					/* checked ri_needLockTagTuple above */
3213					Assert(oldtuple == NULL);
3214
3215					result = ExecUpdateAct(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid,
3216										   NULL, newslot, canSetTag,
3217										   &updateCxt);
3218
3219					/*
3220					 * As in ExecUpdate(), if ExecUpdateAct() reports that a
3221					 * cross-partition update was done, then there's nothing
3222					 * else for us to do --- the UPDATE has been turned into a
3223					 * DELETE and an INSERT, and we must not perform any of
3224					 * the usual post-update tasks.  Also, the RETURNING tuple
3225					 * (if any) has been projected, so we can just return
3226					 * that.
3227					 */
3228					if (updateCxt.crossPartUpdate)
3229					{
3230						mtstate->mt_merge_updated += 1;
3231						rslot = context->cpUpdateReturningSlot;
3232						goto out;
3233					}
3234				}
3235
3236				if (result == TM_Ok)
3237				{
3238					ExecUpdateEpilogue(context, &updateCxt, resultRelInfo,
3239									   tupleid, NULL, newslot);
3240					mtstate->mt_merge_updated += 1;
3241				}
3242				break;
3243
3244			case CMD_DELETE:
3245				mtstate->mt_merge_action = relaction;
3246				if (!ExecDeletePrologue(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid,
3247										NULL, NULL, &result))
3248				{
3249					if (result == TM_Ok)
3250						goto out;	/* "do nothing" */
3251
3252					break;		/* concurrent update/delete */
3253				}
3254
3255				/* INSTEAD OF ROW DELETE Triggers */
3256				if (resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc &&
3257					resultRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc->trig_delete_instead_row)
3258				{
3259					if (!ExecIRDeleteTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo,
3260											  oldtuple))
3261						goto out;	/* "do nothing" */
3262				}
3263				else
3264				{
3265					/* checked ri_needLockTagTuple above */
3266					Assert(oldtuple == NULL);
3267
3268					result = ExecDeleteAct(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid,
3269										   false);
3270				}
3271
3272				if (result == TM_Ok)
3273				{
3274					ExecDeleteEpilogue(context, resultRelInfo, tupleid, NULL,
3275									   false);
3276					mtstate->mt_merge_deleted += 1;
3277				}
3278				break;
3279
3280			case CMD_NOTHING:
3281				/* Doing nothing is always OK */
3282				result = TM_Ok;
3283				break;
3284
3285			default:
3286				elog(ERROR, "unknown action in MERGE WHEN clause");
3287		}
3288
3289		switch (result)
3290		{
3291			case TM_Ok:
3292				/* all good; perform final actions */
3293				if (canSetTag && commandType != CMD_NOTHING)
3294					(estate->es_processed)++;
3295
3296				break;
3297
3298			case TM_SelfModified:
3299
3300				/*
3301				 * The target tuple was already updated or deleted by the
3302				 * current command, or by a later command in the current
3303				 * transaction.  The former case is explicitly disallowed by
3304				 * the SQL standard for MERGE, which insists that the MERGE
3305				 * join condition should not join a target row to more than
3306				 * one source row.
3307				 *
3308				 * The latter case arises if the tuple is modified by a
3309				 * command in a BEFORE trigger, or perhaps by a command in a
3310				 * volatile function used in the query.  In such situations we
3311				 * should not ignore the MERGE action, but it is equally
3312				 * unsafe to proceed.  We don't want to discard the original
3313				 * MERGE action while keeping the triggered actions based on
3314				 * it; and it would be no better to allow the original MERGE
3315				 * action while discarding the updates that it triggered.  So
3316				 * throwing an error is the only safe course.
3317				 */
3318				if (context->tmfd.cmax != estate->es_output_cid)
3319					ereport(ERROR,
3320							(errcode(ERRCODE_TRIGGERED_DATA_CHANGE_VIOLATION),
3321							 errmsg("tuple to be updated or deleted was already modified by an operation triggered by the current command"),
3322							 errhint("Consider using an AFTER trigger instead of a BEFORE trigger to propagate changes to other rows.")));
3323
3324				if (TransactionIdIsCurrentTransactionId(context->tmfd.xmax))
3325					ereport(ERROR,
3326							(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
3327					/* translator: %s is a SQL command name */
3328							 errmsg("%s command cannot affect row a second time",
3329									"MERGE"),
3330							 errhint("Ensure that not more than one source row matches any one target row.")));
3331
3332				/* This shouldn't happen */
3333				elog(ERROR, "attempted to update or delete invisible tuple");
3334				break;
3335
3336			case TM_Deleted:
3337				if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
3338					ereport(ERROR,
3339							(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
3340							 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent delete")));
3341
3342				/*
3343				 * If the tuple was already deleted, set matched to false to
3344				 * let caller handle it under NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] clauses.
3345				 */
3346				*matched = false;
3347				goto out;
3348
3349			case TM_Updated:
3350				{
3351					bool		was_matched;
3352					Relation	resultRelationDesc;
3353					TupleTableSlot *epqslot,
3354							   *inputslot;
3355					LockTupleMode lockmode;
3356
3357					if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
3358						ereport(ERROR,
3359								(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
3360								 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent update")));
3361
3362					/*
3363					 * The target tuple was concurrently updated by some other
3364					 * transaction.  If we are currently processing a MATCHED
3365					 * action, use EvalPlanQual() with the new version of the
3366					 * tuple and recheck the join qual, to detect a change
3367					 * from the MATCHED to the NOT MATCHED cases.  If we are
3368					 * already processing a NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE action, we
3369					 * skip this (cannot switch from NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE to
3370					 * MATCHED).
3371					 */
3372					was_matched = relaction->mas_action->matchKind == MERGE_WHEN_MATCHED;
3373					resultRelationDesc = resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3374					lockmode = ExecUpdateLockMode(estate, resultRelInfo);
3375
3376					if (was_matched)
3377						inputslot = EvalPlanQualSlot(epqstate, resultRelationDesc,
3378													 resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex);
3379					else
3380						inputslot = resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot;
3381
3382					result = table_tuple_lock(resultRelationDesc, tupleid,
3383											  estate->es_snapshot,
3384											  inputslot, estate->es_output_cid,
3385											  lockmode, LockWaitBlock,
3386											  TUPLE_LOCK_FLAG_FIND_LAST_VERSION,
3387											  &context->tmfd);
3388					switch (result)
3389					{
3390						case TM_Ok:
3391
3392							/*
3393							 * If the tuple was updated and migrated to
3394							 * another partition concurrently, the current
3395							 * MERGE implementation can't follow.  There's
3396							 * probably a better way to handle this case, but
3397							 * it'd require recognizing the relation to which
3398							 * the tuple moved, and setting our current
3399							 * resultRelInfo to that.
3400							 */
3401							if (ItemPointerIndicatesMovedPartitions(tupleid))
3402								ereport(ERROR,
3403										(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
3404										 errmsg("tuple to be merged was already moved to another partition due to concurrent update")));
3405
3406							/*
3407							 * If this was a MATCHED case, use EvalPlanQual()
3408							 * to recheck the join condition.
3409							 */
3410							if (was_matched)
3411							{
3412								epqslot = EvalPlanQual(epqstate,
3413													   resultRelationDesc,
3414													   resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex,
3415													   inputslot);
3416
3417								/*
3418								 * If the subplan didn't return a tuple, then
3419								 * we must be dealing with an inner join for
3420								 * which the join condition no longer matches.
3421								 * This can only happen if there are no NOT
3422								 * MATCHED actions, and so there is nothing
3423								 * more to do.
3424								 */
3425								if (TupIsNull(epqslot))
3426									goto out;
3427
3428								/*
3429								 * If we got a NULL ctid from the subplan, the
3430								 * join quals no longer pass and we switch to
3431								 * the NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE case.
3432								 */
3433								(void) ExecGetJunkAttribute(epqslot,
3434															resultRelInfo->ri_RowIdAttNo,
3435															&isNull);
3436								if (isNull)
3437									*matched = false;
3438
3439								/*
3440								 * Otherwise, recheck the join quals to see if
3441								 * we need to switch to the NOT MATCHED BY
3442								 * SOURCE case.
3443								 */
3444								if (resultRelInfo->ri_needLockTagTuple)
3445								{
3446									if (ItemPointerIsValid(&lockedtid))
3447										UnlockTuple(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc, &lockedtid,
3448													InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
3449									LockTuple(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc, tupleid,
3450											  InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
3451									lockedtid = *tupleid;
3452								}
3453
3454								if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(resultRelationDesc,
3455																   tupleid,
3456																   SnapshotAny,
3457																   resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot))
3458									elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch the target tuple");
3459
3460								if (*matched)
3461									*matched = ExecQual(resultRelInfo->ri_MergeJoinCondition,
3462														econtext);
3463
3464								/* Switch lists, if necessary */
3465								if (!*matched)
3466								{
3467									actionStates = mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE];
3468
3469									/*
3470									 * If we have both NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE
3471									 * and NOT MATCHED BY TARGET actions (a
3472									 * full join between the source and target
3473									 * relations), the single previously
3474									 * matched tuple from the outer plan node
3475									 * is treated as two not matched tuples,
3476									 * in the same way as if they had not
3477									 * matched to start with.  Therefore, we
3478									 * must adjust the outer plan node's tuple
3479									 * count, if we're instrumenting the
3480									 * query, to get the correct "skipped" row
3481									 * count --- see show_modifytable_info().
3482									 */
3483									if (outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument &&
3484										mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE] &&
3485										mergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET])
3486										InstrUpdateTupleCount(outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument, 1.0);
3487								}
3488							}
3489
3490							/*
3491							 * Loop back and process the MATCHED or NOT
3492							 * MATCHED BY SOURCE actions from the start.
3493							 */
3494							goto lmerge_matched;
3495
3496						case TM_Deleted:
3497
3498							/*
3499							 * tuple already deleted; tell caller to run NOT
3500							 * MATCHED [BY TARGET] actions
3501							 */
3502							*matched = false;
3503							goto out;
3504
3505						case TM_SelfModified:
3506
3507							/*
3508							 * This can be reached when following an update
3509							 * chain from a tuple updated by another session,
3510							 * reaching a tuple that was already updated or
3511							 * deleted by the current command, or by a later
3512							 * command in the current transaction. As above,
3513							 * this should always be treated as an error.
3514							 */
3515							if (context->tmfd.cmax != estate->es_output_cid)
3516								ereport(ERROR,
3517										(errcode(ERRCODE_TRIGGERED_DATA_CHANGE_VIOLATION),
3518										 errmsg("tuple to be updated or deleted was already modified by an operation triggered by the current command"),
3519										 errhint("Consider using an AFTER trigger instead of a BEFORE trigger to propagate changes to other rows.")));
3520
3521							if (TransactionIdIsCurrentTransactionId(context->tmfd.xmax))
3522								ereport(ERROR,
3523										(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
3524								/* translator: %s is a SQL command name */
3525										 errmsg("%s command cannot affect row a second time",
3526												"MERGE"),
3527										 errhint("Ensure that not more than one source row matches any one target row.")));
3528
3529							/* This shouldn't happen */
3530							elog(ERROR, "attempted to update or delete invisible tuple");
3531							goto out;
3532
3533						default:
3534							/* see table_tuple_lock call in ExecDelete() */
3535							elog(ERROR, "unexpected table_tuple_lock status: %u",
3536								 result);
3537							goto out;
3538					}
3539				}
3540
3541			case TM_Invisible:
3542			case TM_WouldBlock:
3543			case TM_BeingModified:
3544				/* these should not occur */
3545				elog(ERROR, "unexpected tuple operation result: %d", result);
3546				break;
3547		}
3548
3549		/* Process RETURNING if present */
3550		if (resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning)
3551		{
3552			switch (commandType)
3553			{
3554				case CMD_UPDATE:
3555					rslot = ExecProcessReturning(context,
3556												 resultRelInfo,
3557												 CMD_UPDATE,
3558												 resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot,
3559												 newslot,
3560												 context->planSlot);
3561					break;
3562
3563				case CMD_DELETE:
3564					rslot = ExecProcessReturning(context,
3565												 resultRelInfo,
3566												 CMD_DELETE,
3567												 resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot,
3568												 NULL,
3569												 context->planSlot);
3570					break;
3571
3572				case CMD_NOTHING:
3573					break;
3574
3575				default:
3576					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d",
3577						 (int) commandType);
3578			}
3579		}
3580
3581		/*
3582		 * We've activated one of the WHEN clauses, so we don't search
3583		 * further. This is required behaviour, not an optimization.
3584		 */
3585		break;
3586	}
3587
3588	/*
3589	 * Successfully executed an action or no qualifying action was found.
3590	 */
3591out:
3592	if (ItemPointerIsValid(&lockedtid))
3593		UnlockTuple(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc, &lockedtid,
3594					InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
3595	return rslot;
3596}
3597
3598/*
3599 * Execute the first qualifying NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] action.
3600 */
3601static TupleTableSlot *
3602ExecMergeNotMatched(ModifyTableContext *context, ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
3603					bool canSetTag)
3604{
3605	ModifyTableState *mtstate = context->mtstate;
3606	ExprContext *econtext = mtstate->ps.ps_ExprContext;
3607	List	   *actionStates;
3608	TupleTableSlot *rslot = NULL;
3609	ListCell   *l;
3610
3611	/*
3612	 * For INSERT actions, the root relation's merge action is OK since the
3613	 * INSERT's targetlist and the WHEN conditions can only refer to the
3614	 * source relation and hence it does not matter which result relation we
3615	 * work with.
3616	 *
3617	 * XXX does this mean that we can avoid creating copies of actionStates on
3618	 * partitioned tables, for not-matched actions?
3619	 */
3620	actionStates = resultRelInfo->ri_MergeActions[MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET];
3621
3622	/*
3623	 * Make source tuple available to ExecQual and ExecProject. We don't need
3624	 * the target tuple, since the WHEN quals and targetlist can't refer to
3625	 * the target columns.
3626	 */
3627	econtext->ecxt_scantuple = NULL;
3628	econtext->ecxt_innertuple = context->planSlot;
3629	econtext->ecxt_outertuple = NULL;
3630
3631	foreach(l, actionStates)
3632	{
3633		MergeActionState *action = (MergeActionState *) lfirst(l);
3634		CmdType		commandType = action->mas_action->commandType;
3635		TupleTableSlot *newslot;
3636
3637		/*
3638		 * Test condition, if any.
3639		 *
3640		 * In the absence of any condition, we perform the action
3641		 * unconditionally (no need to check separately since ExecQual() will
3642		 * return true if there are no conditions to evaluate).
3643		 */
3644		if (!ExecQual(action->mas_whenqual, econtext))
3645			continue;
3646
3647		/* Perform stated action */
3648		switch (commandType)
3649		{
3650			case CMD_INSERT:
3651
3652				/*
3653				 * Project the tuple.  In case of a partitioned table, the
3654				 * projection was already built to use the root's descriptor,
3655				 * so we don't need to map the tuple here.
3656				 */
3657				newslot = ExecProject(action->mas_proj);
3658				mtstate->mt_merge_action = action;
3659
3660				rslot = ExecInsert(context, mtstate->rootResultRelInfo,
3661								   newslot, canSetTag, NULL, NULL);
3662				mtstate->mt_merge_inserted += 1;
3663				break;
3664			case CMD_NOTHING:
3665				/* Do nothing */
3666				break;
3667			default:
3668				elog(ERROR, "unknown action in MERGE WHEN NOT MATCHED clause");
3669		}
3670
3671		/*
3672		 * We've activated one of the WHEN clauses, so we don't search
3673		 * further. This is required behaviour, not an optimization.
3674		 */
3675		break;
3676	}
3677
3678	return rslot;
3679}
3680
3681/*
3682 * Initialize state for execution of MERGE.
3683 */
3684void
3685ExecInitMerge(ModifyTableState *mtstate, EState *estate)
3686{
3687	List	   *mergeActionLists = mtstate->mt_mergeActionLists;
3688	List	   *mergeJoinConditions = mtstate->mt_mergeJoinConditions;
3689	ResultRelInfo *rootRelInfo = mtstate->rootResultRelInfo;
3690	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo;
3691	ExprContext *econtext;
3692	ListCell   *lc;
3693	int			i;
3694
3695	if (mergeActionLists == NIL)
3696		return;
3697
3698	mtstate->mt_merge_subcommands = 0;
3699
3700	if (mtstate->ps.ps_ExprContext == NULL)
3701		ExecAssignExprContext(estate, &mtstate->ps);
3702	econtext = mtstate->ps.ps_ExprContext;
3703
3704	/*
3705	 * Create a MergeActionState for each action on the mergeActionList and
3706	 * add it to either a list of matched actions or not-matched actions.
3707	 *
3708	 * Similar logic appears in ExecInitPartitionInfo(), so if changing
3709	 * anything here, do so there too.
3710	 */
3711	i = 0;
3712	foreach(lc, mergeActionLists)
3713	{
3714		List	   *mergeActionList = lfirst(lc);
3715		Node	   *joinCondition;
3716		TupleDesc	relationDesc;
3717		ListCell   *l;
3718
3719		joinCondition = (Node *) list_nth(mergeJoinConditions, i);
3720		resultRelInfo = mtstate->resultRelInfo + i;
3721		i++;
3722		relationDesc = RelationGetDescr(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc);
3723
3724		/* initialize slots for MERGE fetches from this rel */
3725		if (unlikely(!resultRelInfo->ri_projectNewInfoValid))
3726			ExecInitMergeTupleSlots(mtstate, resultRelInfo);
3727
3728		/* initialize state for join condition checking */
3729		resultRelInfo->ri_MergeJoinCondition =
3730			ExecInitQual((List *) joinCondition, &mtstate->ps);
3731
3732		foreach(l, mergeActionList)
3733		{
3734			MergeAction *action = (MergeAction *) lfirst(l);
3735			MergeActionState *action_state;
3736			TupleTableSlot *tgtslot;
3737			TupleDesc	tgtdesc;
3738
3739			/*
3740			 * Build action merge state for this rel.  (For partitions,
3741			 * equivalent code exists in ExecInitPartitionInfo.)
3742			 */
3743			action_state = makeNode(MergeActionState);
3744			action_state->mas_action = action;
3745			action_state->mas_whenqual = ExecInitQual((List *) action->qual,
3746													  &mtstate->ps);
3747
3748			/*
3749			 * We create three lists - one for each MergeMatchKind - and stick
3750			 * the MergeActionState into the appropriate list.
3751			 */
3752			resultRelInfo->ri_MergeActions[action->matchKind] =
3753				lappend(resultRelInfo->ri_MergeActions[action->matchKind],
3754						action_state);
3755
3756			switch (action->commandType)
3757			{
3758				case CMD_INSERT:
3759					/* INSERT actions always use rootRelInfo */
3760					ExecCheckPlanOutput(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3761										action->targetList);
3762
3763					/*
3764					 * If the MERGE targets a partitioned table, any INSERT
3765					 * actions must be routed through it, not the child
3766					 * relations. Initialize the routing struct and the root
3767					 * table's "new" tuple slot for that, if not already done.
3768					 * The projection we prepare, for all relations, uses the
3769					 * root relation descriptor, and targets the plan's root
3770					 * slot.  (This is consistent with the fact that we
3771					 * checked the plan output to match the root relation,
3772					 * above.)
3773					 */
3774					if (rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc->rd_rel->relkind ==
3775						RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3776					{
3777						if (mtstate->mt_partition_tuple_routing == NULL)
3778						{
3779							/*
3780							 * Initialize planstate for routing if not already
3781							 * done.
3782							 *
3783							 * Note that the slot is managed as a standalone
3784							 * slot belonging to ModifyTableState, so we pass
3785							 * NULL for the 2nd argument.
3786							 */
3787							mtstate->mt_root_tuple_slot =
3788								table_slot_create(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3789												  NULL);
3790							mtstate->mt_partition_tuple_routing =
3791								ExecSetupPartitionTupleRouting(estate,
3792															   rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc);
3793						}
3794						tgtslot = mtstate->mt_root_tuple_slot;
3795						tgtdesc = RelationGetDescr(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc);
3796					}
3797					else
3798					{
3799						/*
3800						 * If the MERGE targets an inherited table, we insert
3801						 * into the root table, so we must initialize its
3802						 * "new" tuple slot, if not already done, and use its
3803						 * relation descriptor for the projection.
3804						 *
3805						 * For non-inherited tables, rootRelInfo and
3806						 * resultRelInfo are the same, and the "new" tuple
3807						 * slot will already have been initialized.
3808						 */
3809						if (rootRelInfo->ri_newTupleSlot == NULL)
3810							rootRelInfo->ri_newTupleSlot =
3811								table_slot_create(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3812												  &estate->es_tupleTable);
3813
3814						tgtslot = rootRelInfo->ri_newTupleSlot;
3815						tgtdesc = RelationGetDescr(rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc);
3816					}
3817
3818					action_state->mas_proj =
3819						ExecBuildProjectionInfo(action->targetList, econtext,
3820												tgtslot,
3821												&mtstate->ps,
3822												tgtdesc);
3823
3824					mtstate->mt_merge_subcommands |= MERGE_INSERT;
3825					break;
3826				case CMD_UPDATE:
3827					action_state->mas_proj =
3828						ExecBuildUpdateProjection(action->targetList,
3829												  true,
3830												  action->updateColnos,
3831												  relationDesc,
3832												  econtext,
3833												  resultRelInfo->ri_newTupleSlot,
3834												  &mtstate->ps);
3835					mtstate->mt_merge_subcommands |= MERGE_UPDATE;
3836					break;
3837				case CMD_DELETE:
3838					mtstate->mt_merge_subcommands |= MERGE_DELETE;
3839					break;
3840				case CMD_NOTHING:
3841					break;
3842				default:
3843					elog(ERROR, "unknown action in MERGE WHEN clause");
3844					break;
3845			}
3846		}
3847	}
3848
3849	/*
3850	 * If the MERGE targets an inherited table, any INSERT actions will use
3851	 * rootRelInfo, and rootRelInfo will not be in the resultRelInfo array.
3852	 * Therefore we must initialize its WITH CHECK OPTION constraints and
3853	 * RETURNING projection, as ExecInitModifyTable did for the resultRelInfo
3854	 * entries.
3855	 *
3856	 * Note that the planner does not build a withCheckOptionList or
3857	 * returningList for the root relation, but as in ExecInitPartitionInfo,
3858	 * we can use the first resultRelInfo entry as a reference to calculate
3859	 * the attno's for the root table.
3860	 */
3861	if (rootRelInfo != mtstate->resultRelInfo &&
3862		rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE &&
3863		(mtstate->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_INSERT) != 0)
3864	{
3865		ModifyTable *node = (ModifyTable *) mtstate->ps.plan;
3866		Relation	rootRelation = rootRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3867		Relation	firstResultRel = mtstate->resultRelInfo[0].ri_RelationDesc;
3868		int			firstVarno = mtstate->resultRelInfo[0].ri_RangeTableIndex;
3869		AttrMap    *part_attmap = NULL;
3870		bool		found_whole_row;
3871
3872		if (node->withCheckOptionLists != NIL)
3873		{
3874			List	   *wcoList;
3875			List	   *wcoExprs = NIL;
3876
3877			/* There should be as many WCO lists as result rels */
3878			Assert(list_length(node->withCheckOptionLists) ==
3879				   list_length(node->resultRelations));
3880
3881			/*
3882			 * Use the first WCO list as a reference. In the most common case,
3883			 * this will be for the same relation as rootRelInfo, and so there
3884			 * will be no need to adjust its attno's.
3885			 */
3886			wcoList = linitial(node->withCheckOptionLists);
3887			if (rootRelation != firstResultRel)
3888			{
3889				/* Convert any Vars in it to contain the root's attno's */
3890				part_attmap =
3891					build_attrmap_by_name(RelationGetDescr(rootRelation),
3892										  RelationGetDescr(firstResultRel),
3893										  false);
3894
3895				wcoList = (List *)
3896					map_variable_attnos((Node *) wcoList,
3897										firstVarno, 0,
3898										part_attmap,
3899										RelationGetForm(rootRelation)->reltype,
3900										&found_whole_row);
3901			}
3902
3903			foreach(lc, wcoList)
3904			{
3905				WithCheckOption *wco = lfirst_node(WithCheckOption, lc);
3906				ExprState  *wcoExpr = ExecInitQual(castNode(List, wco->qual),
3907												   &mtstate->ps);
3908
3909				wcoExprs = lappend(wcoExprs, wcoExpr);
3910			}
3911
3912			rootRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptions = wcoList;
3913			rootRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptionExprs = wcoExprs;
3914		}
3915
3916		if (node->returningLists != NIL)
3917		{
3918			List	   *returningList;
3919
3920			/* There should be as many returning lists as result rels */
3921			Assert(list_length(node->returningLists) ==
3922				   list_length(node->resultRelations));
3923
3924			/*
3925			 * Use the first returning list as a reference. In the most common
3926			 * case, this will be for the same relation as rootRelInfo, and so
3927			 * there will be no need to adjust its attno's.
3928			 */
3929			returningList = linitial(node->returningLists);
3930			if (rootRelation != firstResultRel)
3931			{
3932				/* Convert any Vars in it to contain the root's attno's */
3933				if (part_attmap == NULL)
3934					part_attmap =
3935						build_attrmap_by_name(RelationGetDescr(rootRelation),
3936											  RelationGetDescr(firstResultRel),
3937											  false);
3938
3939				returningList = (List *)
3940					map_variable_attnos((Node *) returningList,
3941										firstVarno, 0,
3942										part_attmap,
3943										RelationGetForm(rootRelation)->reltype,
3944										&found_whole_row);
3945			}
3946			rootRelInfo->ri_returningList = returningList;
3947
3948			/* Initialize the RETURNING projection */
3949			rootRelInfo->ri_projectReturning =
3950				ExecBuildProjectionInfo(returningList, econtext,
3951										mtstate->ps.ps_ResultTupleSlot,
3952										&mtstate->ps,
3953										RelationGetDescr(rootRelation));
3954		}
3955	}
3956}
3957
3958/*
3959 * Initializes the tuple slots in a ResultRelInfo for any MERGE action.
3960 *
3961 * We mark 'projectNewInfoValid' even though the projections themselves
3962 * are not initialized here.
3963 */
3964void
3965ExecInitMergeTupleSlots(ModifyTableState *mtstate,
3966						ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo)
3967{
3968	EState	   *estate = mtstate->ps.state;
3969
3970	Assert(!resultRelInfo->ri_projectNewInfoValid);
3971
3972	resultRelInfo->ri_oldTupleSlot =
3973		table_slot_create(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3974						  &estate->es_tupleTable);
3975	resultRelInfo->ri_newTupleSlot =
3976		table_slot_create(resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3977						  &estate->es_tupleTable);
3978	resultRelInfo->ri_projectNewInfoValid = true;
3979}
3980
3981/*
3982 * Process BEFORE EACH STATEMENT triggers
3983 */
3984static void
3985fireBSTriggers(ModifyTableState *node)
3986{
3987	ModifyTable *plan = (ModifyTable *) node->ps.plan;
3988	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo = node->rootResultRelInfo;
3989
3990	switch (node->operation)
3991	{
3992		case CMD_INSERT:
3993			ExecBSInsertTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
3994			if (plan->onConflictAction == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
3995				ExecBSUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state,
3996									 resultRelInfo);
3997			break;
3998		case CMD_UPDATE:
3999			ExecBSUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
4000			break;
4001		case CMD_DELETE:
4002			ExecBSDeleteTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
4003			break;
4004		case CMD_MERGE:
4005			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_INSERT)
4006				ExecBSInsertTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
4007			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_UPDATE)
4008				ExecBSUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
4009			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_DELETE)
4010				ExecBSDeleteTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo);
4011			break;
4012		default:
4013			elog(ERROR, "unknown operation");
4014			break;
4015	}
4016}
4017
4018/*
4019 * Process AFTER EACH STATEMENT triggers
4020 */
4021static void
4022fireASTriggers(ModifyTableState *node)
4023{
4024	ModifyTable *plan = (ModifyTable *) node->ps.plan;
4025	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo = node->rootResultRelInfo;
4026
4027	switch (node->operation)
4028	{
4029		case CMD_INSERT:
4030			if (plan->onConflictAction == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
4031				ExecASUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state,
4032									 resultRelInfo,
4033									 node->mt_oc_transition_capture);
4034			ExecASInsertTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4035								 node->mt_transition_capture);
4036			break;
4037		case CMD_UPDATE:
4038			ExecASUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4039								 node->mt_transition_capture);
4040			break;
4041		case CMD_DELETE:
4042			ExecASDeleteTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4043								 node->mt_transition_capture);
4044			break;
4045		case CMD_MERGE:
4046			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_DELETE)
4047				ExecASDeleteTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4048									 node->mt_transition_capture);
4049			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_UPDATE)
4050				ExecASUpdateTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4051									 node->mt_transition_capture);
4052			if (node->mt_merge_subcommands & MERGE_INSERT)
4053				ExecASInsertTriggers(node->ps.state, resultRelInfo,
4054									 node->mt_transition_capture);
4055			break;
4056		default:
4057			elog(ERROR, "unknown operation");
4058			break;
4059	}
4060}
4061
4062/*
4063 * Set up the state needed for collecting transition tuples for AFTER
4064 * triggers.
4065 */
4066static void
4067ExecSetupTransitionCaptureState(ModifyTableState *mtstate, EState *estate)
4068{
4069	ModifyTable *plan = (ModifyTable *) mtstate->ps.plan;
4070	ResultRelInfo *targetRelInfo = mtstate->rootResultRelInfo;
4071
4072	/* Check for transition tables on the directly targeted relation. */
4073	mtstate->mt_transition_capture =
4074		MakeTransitionCaptureState(targetRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc,
4075								   RelationGetRelid(targetRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc),
4076								   mtstate->operation);
4077	if (plan->operation == CMD_INSERT &&
4078		plan->onConflictAction == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
4079		mtstate->mt_oc_transition_capture =
4080			MakeTransitionCaptureState(targetRelInfo->ri_TrigDesc,
4081									   RelationGetRelid(targetRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc),
4082									   CMD_UPDATE);
4083}
4084
4085/*
4086 * ExecPrepareTupleRouting --- prepare for routing one tuple
4087 *
4088 * Determine the partition in which the tuple in slot is to be inserted,
4089 * and return its ResultRelInfo in *partRelInfo.  The return value is
4090 * a slot holding the tuple of the partition rowtype.
4091 *
4092 * This also sets the transition table information in mtstate based on the
4093 * selected partition.
4094 */
4095static TupleTableSlot *
4096ExecPrepareTupleRouting(ModifyTableState *mtstate,
4097						EState *estate,
4098						PartitionTupleRouting *proute,
4099						ResultRelInfo *targetRelInfo,
4100						TupleTableSlot *slot,
4101						ResultRelInfo **partRelInfo)
4102{
4103	ResultRelInfo *partrel;
4104	TupleConversionMap *map;
4105
4106	/*
4107	 * Lookup the target partition's ResultRelInfo.  If ExecFindPartition does
4108	 * not find a valid partition for the tuple in 'slot' then an error is
4109	 * raised.  An error may also be raised if the found partition is not a
4110	 * valid target for INSERTs.  This is required since a partitioned table
4111	 * UPDATE to another partition becomes a DELETE+INSERT.
4112	 */
4113	partrel = ExecFindPartition(mtstate, targetRelInfo, proute, slot, estate);
4114
4115	/*
4116	 * If we're capturing transition tuples, we might need to convert from the
4117	 * partition rowtype to root partitioned table's rowtype.  But if there
4118	 * are no BEFORE triggers on the partition that could change the tuple, we
4119	 * can just remember the original unconverted tuple to avoid a needless
4120	 * round trip conversion.
4121	 */
4122	if (mtstate->mt_transition_capture != NULL)
4123	{
4124		bool		has_before_insert_row_trig;
4125
4126		has_before_insert_row_trig = (partrel->ri_TrigDesc &&
4127									  partrel->ri_TrigDesc->trig_insert_before_row);
4128
4129		mtstate->mt_transition_capture->tcs_original_insert_tuple =
4130			!has_before_insert_row_trig ? slot : NULL;
4131	}
4132
4133	/*
4134	 * Convert the tuple, if necessary.
4135	 */
4136	map = ExecGetRootToChildMap(partrel, estate);
4137	if (map != NULL)
4138	{
4139		TupleTableSlot *new_slot = partrel->ri_PartitionTupleSlot;
4140
4141		slot = execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap, slot, new_slot);
4142	}
4143
4144	*partRelInfo = partrel;
4145	return slot;
4146}
4147
4148/* ----------------------------------------------------------------
4149 *	   ExecModifyTable
4150 *
4151 *		Perform table modifications as required, and return RETURNING results
4152 *		if needed.
4153 * ----------------------------------------------------------------
4154 */
4155static TupleTableSlot *
4156ExecModifyTable(PlanState *pstate)
4157{
4158	ModifyTableState *node = castNode(ModifyTableState, pstate);
4159	ModifyTableContext context;
4160	EState	   *estate = node->ps.state;
4161	CmdType		operation = node->operation;
4162	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo;
4163	PlanState  *subplanstate;
4164	TupleTableSlot *slot;
4165	TupleTableSlot *oldSlot;
4166	ItemPointerData tuple_ctid;
4167	HeapTupleData oldtupdata;
4168	HeapTuple	oldtuple;
4169	ItemPointer tupleid;
4170	bool		tuplock;
4171
4172	CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
4173
4174	/*
4175	 * This should NOT get called during EvalPlanQual; we should have passed a
4176	 * subplan tree to EvalPlanQual, instead.  Use a runtime test not just
4177	 * Assert because this condition is easy to miss in testing.  (Note:
4178	 * although ModifyTable should not get executed within an EvalPlanQual
4179	 * operation, we do have to allow it to be initialized and shut down in
4180	 * case it is within a CTE subplan.  Hence this test must be here, not in
4181	 * ExecInitModifyTable.)
4182	 */
4183	if (estate->es_epq_active != NULL)
4184		elog(ERROR, "ModifyTable should not be called during EvalPlanQual");
4185
4186	/*
4187	 * If we've already completed processing, don't try to do more.  We need
4188	 * this test because ExecPostprocessPlan might call us an extra time, and
4189	 * our subplan's nodes aren't necessarily robust against being called
4190	 * extra times.
4191	 */
4192	if (node->mt_done)
4193		return NULL;
4194
4195	/*
4196	 * On first call, fire BEFORE STATEMENT triggers before proceeding.
4197	 */
4198	if (node->fireBSTriggers)
4199	{
4200		fireBSTriggers(node);
4201		node->fireBSTriggers = false;
4202	}
4203
4204	/* Preload local variables */
4205	resultRelInfo = node->resultRelInfo + node->mt_lastResultIndex;
4206	subplanstate = outerPlanState(node);
4207
4208	/* Set global context */
4209	context.mtstate = node;
4210	context.epqstate = &node->mt_epqstate;
4211	context.estate = estate;
4212
4213	/*
4214	 * Fetch rows from subplan, and execute the required table modification
4215	 * for each row.
4216	 */
4217	for (;;)
4218	{
4219		/*
4220		 * Reset the per-output-tuple exprcontext.  This is needed because
4221		 * triggers expect to use that context as workspace.  It's a bit ugly
4222		 * to do this below the top level of the plan, however.  We might need
4223		 * to rethink this later.
4224		 */
4225		ResetPerTupleExprContext(estate);
4226
4227		/*
4228		 * Reset per-tuple memory context used for processing on conflict and
4229		 * returning clauses, to free any expression evaluation storage
4230		 * allocated in the previous cycle.
4231		 */
4232		if (pstate->ps_ExprContext)
4233			ResetExprContext(pstate->ps_ExprContext);
4234
4235		/*
4236		 * If there is a pending MERGE ... WHEN NOT MATCHED [BY TARGET] action
4237		 * to execute, do so now --- see the comments in ExecMerge().
4238		 */
4239		if (node->mt_merge_pending_not_matched != NULL)
4240		{
4241			context.planSlot = node->mt_merge_pending_not_matched;
4242			context.cpDeletedSlot = NULL;
4243
4244			slot = ExecMergeNotMatched(&context, node->resultRelInfo,
4245									   node->canSetTag);
4246
4247			/* Clear the pending action */
4248			node->mt_merge_pending_not_matched = NULL;
4249
4250			/*
4251			 * If we got a RETURNING result, return it to the caller.  We'll
4252			 * continue the work on next call.
4253			 */
4254			if (slot)
4255				return slot;
4256
4257			continue;			/* continue with the next tuple */
4258		}
4259
4260		/* Fetch the next row from subplan */
4261		context.planSlot = ExecProcNode(subplanstate);
4262		context.cpDeletedSlot = NULL;
4263
4264		/* No more tuples to process? */
4265		if (TupIsNull(context.planSlot))
4266			break;
4267
4268		/*
4269		 * When there are multiple result relations, each tuple contains a
4270		 * junk column that gives the OID of the rel from which it came.
4271		 * Extract it and select the correct result relation.
4272		 */
4273		if (AttributeNumberIsValid(node->mt_resultOidAttno))
4274		{
4275			Datum		datum;
4276			bool		isNull;
4277			Oid			resultoid;
4278
4279			datum = ExecGetJunkAttribute(context.planSlot, node->mt_resultOidAttno,
4280										 &isNull);
4281			if (isNull)
4282			{
4283				/*
4284				 * For commands other than MERGE, any tuples having InvalidOid
4285				 * for tableoid are errors.  For MERGE, we may need to handle
4286				 * them as WHEN NOT MATCHED clauses if any, so do that.
4287				 *
4288				 * Note that we use the node's toplevel resultRelInfo, not any
4289				 * specific partition's.
4290				 */
4291				if (operation == CMD_MERGE)
4292				{
4293					EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&node->mt_epqstate, context.planSlot);
4294
4295					slot = ExecMerge(&context, node->resultRelInfo,
4296									 NULL, NULL, node->canSetTag);
4297
4298					/*
4299					 * If we got a RETURNING result, return it to the caller.
4300					 * We'll continue the work on next call.
4301					 */
4302					if (slot)
4303						return slot;
4304
4305					continue;	/* continue with the next tuple */
4306				}
4307
4308				elog(ERROR, "tableoid is NULL");
4309			}
4310			resultoid = DatumGetObjectId(datum);
4311
4312			/* If it's not the same as last time, we need to locate the rel */
4313			if (resultoid != node->mt_lastResultOid)
4314				resultRelInfo = ExecLookupResultRelByOid(node, resultoid,
4315														 false, true);
4316		}
4317
4318		/*
4319		 * If resultRelInfo->ri_usesFdwDirectModify is true, all we need to do
4320		 * here is compute the RETURNING expressions.
4321		 */
4322		if (resultRelInfo->ri_usesFdwDirectModify)
4323		{
4324			Assert(resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning);
4325
4326			/*
4327			 * A scan slot containing the data that was actually inserted,
4328			 * updated or deleted has already been made available to
4329			 * ExecProcessReturning by IterateDirectModify, so no need to
4330			 * provide it here.  The individual old and new slots are not
4331			 * needed, since direct-modify is disabled if the RETURNING list
4332			 * refers to OLD/NEW values.
4333			 */
4334			Assert((resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning->pi_state.flags & EEO_FLAG_HAS_OLD) == 0 &&
4335				   (resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning->pi_state.flags & EEO_FLAG_HAS_NEW) == 0);
4336
4337			slot = ExecProcessReturning(&context, resultRelInfo, operation,
4338										NULL, NULL, context.planSlot);
4339
4340			return slot;
4341		}
4342
4343		EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&node->mt_epqstate, context.planSlot);
4344		slot = context.planSlot;
4345
4346		tupleid = NULL;
4347		oldtuple = NULL;
4348
4349		/*
4350		 * For UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE, fetch the row identity info for the tuple
4351		 * to be updated/deleted/merged.  For a heap relation, that's a TID;
4352		 * otherwise we may have a wholerow junk attr that carries the old
4353		 * tuple in toto.  Keep this in step with the part of
4354		 * ExecInitModifyTable that sets up ri_RowIdAttNo.
4355		 */
4356		if (operation == CMD_UPDATE || operation == CMD_DELETE ||
4357			operation == CMD_MERGE)
4358		{
4359			char		relkind;
4360			Datum		datum;
4361			bool		isNull;
4362
4363			relkind = resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc->rd_rel->relkind;
4364			if (relkind == RELKIND_RELATION ||
4365				relkind == RELKIND_MATVIEW ||
